{{Quote|Its Dren! Dren's firing at us! What's happening?|Evenyl Yob|<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />}}
'''Evenyl Yob''' was a teenage [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] from [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] who served as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps]]' [[Echo Flight]].<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />

==Biography==
===Briefing===
In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], [[Station TFP-9]] was [[Battle at TFP-9|attacked]] by what was assumed to be [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] consisting of [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] with a [[Hornet-class carrier]] serving as their [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]. She was assigned as [[Echo One]] for a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of twelve [[N-X Police Cruiser/Legends|N-X Police Cruisers]]. She would be watched over by two [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighters]] from [[Bravo Squadron/Legends|Bravo]] [[Bravo Seven|Seven]], piloted by [[Essara Till/Legends|Essara Till]] and [[Bravo Eight|Eight]], piloted by [[Dren Melne]], who was secretly contracted by the [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Challep]] to steal both N-1s and some of the cruisers.<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />

===Battle===
When the Naboo fighters arrived both squadrons engaged and drove the Z-95s to their carrier with Echo flight taking minor damage. She was supposed to report the latest development, only for [[Rhys Dallows]] to beat her to it instead. Till then asked her and [[Echo Two (Echo Flight)|Echo Two]], piloted by [[Kerl]], to join her and Dren for an attack run on the Carrier. It was then that more Z-95s arrived, making Yob think that her [[tactical computer/Legends|tactical computer]] malfunctioned; a second Hornet-class Carrier, ''[[Velumina]]'', lead by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sorran]] arrived with more Z-95s, these armed with [[Concussion missile/Legends|Concussion missiles]]. The first carrier dropped [[Droid starfighter/Legends|Droid]] [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />

It was then that Dren Melne betrayed Naboo by breaking formation and opened fire on Echo Two causing the fighter to spiral; despite Yob's reflexes and immediate reaction, the craft collided with her's destroying her [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]]. While Yob desperately called Kerl, Dren proceeded to engage her, demanding Echo Flight to power down their craft. Yob screamed to Till for help; despite losing her droid, Yob pulled impressive evasive maneuvers to keep Dren from locking on, trying to give Till time to stop him. Evenyl was sobbing and screaming over the radio, and son her fighter was hit by one of Dren{{'s}} two [[Proton torpedo/Legends|torpedoes]]; Till managed to shoot one but did not have enough lasers for both. The impact overloaded the craft{{'s}} [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] and the secondary systems started to malfunction. Till ordered Evenyl to power down all systems, leaving her stranded in space. She reported her situation as such when Till regrouped the flight.<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />

In the end, the battle ended in Naboo{{'s}} favor as Essara Till, along with Rhys, managed to destroy the first Hornet-Class carrier and Dren was soon killed by his own employers for failing to deliver what he promised. After the battle [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] from the Station TFP-9 recovered the stranded pilots, but only 5 made it back home alive. Essara Till then gave [[Ric Olié/Legends|Ric Olié]] Evenyl Yob, along with Rhys Dallows and [[Keela Egast]] a recommendation to be part of Bravo flight.<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Hey! I'm supposed to give the status report!|Evenyl Yob|<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />}}
Evenyl Yob, although a young girl, believed that she was supposed to give updates on the status on Echo Flight when Rhys Dallows brought Essara Till up to speed during the battle. When Dren Melne opened fire on her fighter craft, she was screaming and was almost consumed by fear, begging Till to save her. Despite this and her astromech droid being destroyed, she pulled evasive maneuvers on her cruiser keeping Dren from locking on to her craft. This impressed Till who promised to herself that she would commend her when the situation was over.<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />

When her craft was hit and her systems malfunctioned, she was able to power down her fighter at Essara{{'s}} order, despite crying over her radio frequency. Soon she was able to regain her composure enough to report the condition of her ship to notify that she was out of the fight but still alive.<ref name="The Starfighter Trap" />

==Behind the scenes==
Evenyl Yob appears in the short story ''[[The Starfighter Trap]]'', released in [[2000]] on the main website, and on [[Star Wars Gamer 1]].
